prompts using <code>Einstein GEMINI-2.5</code></p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request Limits</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>IDP fails the execution when the number of requests exceeds the Einstein rate limit for the Salesforce Org</p><div><ul><li><p>When using Einstein and <strong>Customize Schema</strong> is enabled: 300 requests per minute</p></li><li><p>When using Einstein and <strong>Customize Schema</strong> is disabled: (300 / number Einstein of prompts in document action) requests per minute</p></li></ul></div></li><li><p>100 concurrent jobs per minute</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Document Languages</p></td><td><div><div><p>Language support varies depending on the selected large language model (LLM) in document actions. For a list of supported languages by provider, see:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="https://help.openai.com/en/articles/8357869-how-to-change-your-language-setting-in-chatgpt#h_513834920e">OpenAIâs supported languages</a></p></li><li><p><a href="https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/models#supported-languages">Geminiâs supported languages</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Polling Limits</p></td><td><div><div><p>10-second interval for retrieving results<br>Applications must avoid polling for results more often than every 10 seconds.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Checkboxes</p></td><td><p>Accuracy of checkbox processing varies by model depending on form complexity. Test thoroughly on many representative documents to confirm acceptable performance for your use case. See <a href="supported-models">Supported Models</a> for more information about how each model performs when processing checkboxes.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Token Usage and Estimation

A token is a unit of text that a large language model (LLM) processes. Tokens are essential for
breaking down input data into manageable pieces that can be analyzed and generated. These are some
estimated conversions that might help understand token lengths:

- 1 token ~= 4 characters
- 1 token ~= Â¾ words
- 1-2 sentences ~= 30 tokens
- 1,500 words ~= 2048 tokens

When analyzing images, each model uses a different tokenization strategy that affects how they
calculate tokens. Refer to each providerâs documentation for additional guidance:

- [Gemini: token usage estimation for images](https://docs.cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/multimodal/image-understanding)
- [OpenAI: token usage estimation for images](https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/images-vision)

## Document Retention Policy

IDP retains the document files and extracts data as needed to process the document extractions. IDP
uses the following features to enhance data retention:

- Document Action Editor

  IDP temporarily stores the files you modify in the editor during the testing process and
  immediately removes these files after you close the editor. IDP also removes the extracted data
  and the files when you end the session or upload a new file.

- Document Action Execution Endpoint

  When you use the execution endpoint API, IDP stores the files in an S3 bucket and stores the data
  extracted during the execution securely.

For all successful executions, IDP retains this data for 24 hours. For executions that require human
review, IDP retains this data for seven days after the user task is completed. IDP retains any
incomplete tasks for 30 days. Currently, you cannot configure these retention periods.

## Data Residency Policy:

IDP uses AWS services such as S3 and RDS to store files and the extracted data. IDP currently
supports 2 regions: US (Virginia) and EU (Frankfurt, Germany). IDP stores the data closest to your
Anypoint control plane region to ensure GDPR requirements. For example, if your Anypoint control
plane runs in the EU region, IDP stores your data in the Frankfurt data center.

## See Also

- [Automation Credits Usage and Rates](../general/automation-credits-usage-and-rates)
- [Supported Models](supported-models)
---

# IDP Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

## June 5, 2026

## Whatâs New

- You can now use Claude Sonnet 4.6 to analyze your documents.

  For additional details on model support and performance, see [Supported Models](supported-models).

- You can now view published document action versions.

  Open earlier versions of your document actions to review their configuration without making
  changes. For additional details and instructions, see
  [View Published Document Action Versions](publishing-document-actions#view-published-versions).

## May 5, 2026

## Whatâs New

- You can now use OpenAI GPT 5.1 and 5.2 to analyze your documents.

  For additional details on model support and performance, see [Supported Models](supported-models).

- You can now search and filter document actions in the **Document Actions** page.

## April 2, 2026

## Whatâs New

- Updated OpenAI model names.
  - Einstein OpenAI GPT-4o Mini 0718 is now Einstein OpenAI GPT-4o Mini.
  - Einstein OpenAI GPT-4o 0806 is now Einstein OpenAI GPT-4o.

## March 20, 2026

## Whatâs New

- Removed Gemini 3 model support.

  This feature isnât quite ready, so weâre removing it for now. Weâll let you know when itâs back
  up.

## March 10, 2026

## Whatâs New

- You can now enable location callouts in Generic document actions with customized schemas.

  For additional details about supported models and limitations, see
  [Configure Document Action Settings](creating-document-actions#document-action-settings)

- You can now select Googleâs GEMINI-3.0 Flash (Beta) model to analyze your documents.

  For additional usage details and considerations about this model, see
  [Supported Models](supported-models)

## October 29, 2025

## Whatâs New

- You can now select Googleâs GEMINI-2.5 Flash model to analyze your documents.

## June 3, 2025

## Whatâs New

- You can now rotate documents in the document action builder preview and review task preview.

  The rotation is for visual assistance and does not modify the original file.

## Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>For the <strong>emails</strong> field in Purchase Order and Invoice actions, IDP now returns <code>null</code> instead of an empty array <code>[]</code> in the execution response after reviewing the associated task.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-17330495</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## May 20, 2025

## Whatâs New

- You can now **Submit** review tasks without having to scroll to the end of the fields list,
  improving accessibility.

## May 8, 2025

## Whatâs New

- You can now enable image recognition in your document actions.

  To enable this feature, see
  [Configure Document Action Settings](analyzing-documents-with-einstein#settings).

- You can now select Googleâs GEMINI-2.0 Flash 001 model to analyze your documents.

  To select the model to use, see
  [Configure Document Action Settings](analyzing-documents-with-einstein#settings).

  For additional information about supported models, see [Supported Models](supported-models)

- Einstein prompts now support up to 128,000 input tokens per request.

  For additional details about token consumption, see
  [Token Usage and Estimation](quotas-and-limits#tokens).

- Data retention policies are updated:
  - For all successful executions, IDP now retains the data for 24 hours.
  - IDP now retains any incomplete tasks for 30 days.

## February 5, 2025

## Whatâs New

- You can now analyze any type of document and fully customize the output structure by creating a
  Generic document action and enabling **Customize Schema**.

  For more information, see
  [Analyzing Documents With Custom User-Defined Schemas](analyzing-documents-with-einstein).
  - Generic document actions now support using non-English characters for prompt instructions when
    **Customize Schema** is enabled.

- New document actions now support the `GPT-4o (gpt-4o-2024-08-06)` and
  `GPT-4o Mini (gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18)` LLM Models.
- New document actions now support masking any personally identifiable information from your
  documents before sending it to the predictive model.
- The API portal documentation for IDP assets published to Exchange now includes the `valueOnly`
  query parameter.
- You can now upload files in Base64 encoding for versioned action executions.

  For an example API call, see
  [Upload a Base64-encoded File](automate-document-processing-with-the-idp-api#upload-base64-file).

- IDP can now detect signatures in document actions that use Einstein.

## Fixed Issues

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Issue Resolution</th><th>ID</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Completed executions no longer stay in status <code>ACKNOWLEDGED</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-17064164</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Failed executions that include Einstein prompts no longer show status <code>IN_PROGRESS</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-17465241</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Required table columns are now shown in the corresponding page during reviews.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>W-17485449</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## November 20, 2024

## Whatâs New

- IDP now fully supports analyzing documents in French, Italian, and Portuguese.
- Organizations with Salesforce Einstein enabled now use Einstein as the default option for
  answering prompts.
- IDP now supports non-latin characters in the file preview during document action creation.

## November 7, 2024

## Whatâs New

- You can now analyze your documents with Salesforce Einstein.

  Use Einstein to analyze documents that donât use a standard format or are difficult to read
  without performing a complex analysis of the extracted data.

  See [Analyze Documents with Einstein](creating-document-actions#einstein) for information about
  configuring Einstein in your document actions.

## October 2, 2024

## Whatâs New

- IDP now supports analyzing TIFF files.

## September 26, 2024

## Whatâs New

- Invoice and Purchase Order data extraction accuracy is improved for all supported languages.
- Invoice and Purchase Order document actions now support extracting VAT and VAT Rate from tables.

## August 22, 2024

## Whatâs New

- IDP now fully supports analyzing documents in Spanish and German.

## May 8, 2024

## Whatâs New

- You can now define a callback URL for your executions.

  See [Define a Callback URL](automate-document-processing-with-the-idp-api#callback-url) for
  details and configuration instructions.

## April 29, 2024

## Whatâs New

- MuleSoft announces the release of Intelligent Document Processing (IDP).

## See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [MuleSoft Services](https://www.mulesoft.com/support-and-services)
- [IDP Overview](./)
---

# Reviewing Processed Documents

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

After processing a document, IDP queues it for review if:

- At least one field marked as _visible_ shows a confidence score lower than the configured
  threshold.
- At least one field marked as _visible_ and _required_ is missing or could not be extracted.

In this case, a user must review the document to verify the values for the conflicting fields. After
the review is finished, the status of the extraction changes to `SUCCEEDED`.

## Before You Begin

Before you can review a document, ensure that:

- You are assigned as a reviewer in the corresponding document action.

## Review a Document in the UI

To review the results of a processed document:

1.  In the navigation panel, click **Review Tasks**.
2.  Click the name of the document to review.
3.  In the **Results** panel, verify the values extracted from each field.

    During this step, you can modify the values that require adjustment.

    IDP highlights fields in yellow when the extracted values have a confidence score below the
    configured threshold.

4.  After reviewing all values, click **Submit This Page**.

    If there are more than one results page, click **Submit and Next** and continue the process
    until you review all pages.

## See Also

- [Processing Documents and Retrieving Results With the API](automate-document-processing-with-the-idp-api)
- [Processing Documents and Retrieving Results With RPA](automate-document-processing-with-rpa)
- [Adding Reviewers](adding-reviewers)
---

# Security Best Practices

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Securing your IDP implementation requires an intermediary API layer to enforce granular access
control, along with governance practices for reviewer access. These measures help align with
enterprise security standards for Non-Human Accounts, Separation of Duties, and Change Management.
Native IDP access is controlled at the Anypoint Platform Business Group level, which means any
connected app within that organizational unit can invoke any document action or version.

To enforce granular access control, implement two intermediary APIs in each Business Group: an
Anypoint IDP System API (SAPI) for authorization and an Anypoint IDP Call-Back Experience API (XAPI)
for callback authentication.

## Anypoint IDP SAPI and ACL Management

The primary purpose of the SAPI is to provide a granular authorization layer and environment
separation.

### Granular Authorization via ACL

The SAPI references an Access Control List (ACL) maintained in a Postgres database such as AWS
Aurora.

- Each client (consumer) must request its own unique client credentials for the SAPI in the Anypoint
  Exchange for both production and non-production environments.
- At runtime, the SAPI extracts the `client_id` from the Okta JWT bearer token and validates it
  against the ACL.
- The SAPI ensures that the specific client is authorized for the requested `actionId` AND
  `actionVersion`.
- If not authorized, the SAPI returns a `403 Forbidden` error.

### Environment Separation (SIT to Production)

Because IDP uses a single design/runtime environment, the SAPI facilitates System Integration
Testing (SIT):

- Map SIT-specific credentials to a test version of a document action in the ACL.
- Keep production credentials locked to a stable version.
- After testing is complete, issue new production credentials and disable the SIT credentials.

### Cryptographic Nonce Generation

When the SAPI forwards a valid request to IDP, it also generates a cryptographic nonce (key), stores
the nonce in an Anypoint Object Store, and adds a cross-reference to the documentâs execution ID.
This is the foundation for securing the callback.

## Anypoint IDP Call-Back XAPI

By design, the native callback URL from IDP to your solution cannot support client authentication
(OAuth) or IP restrictions. The XAPI authenticates these callback requests.

### Nonce Validation

When IDP finishes processing, it calls the XAPI. The nonce generated by the SAPI is included in this
call as a query parameter. The XAPI validates this nonce against the execution ID in the Object
Store.

### Error Handling

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Scenario</th><th>Response</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Nonce is not recognized</p></td><td><p><code>401 Unauthorized</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Nonce is recognized but does not correlate to the specific execution ID</p></td><td><p><code>403 Forbidden</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Downstream Delivery

If authorized, the XAPI retrieves the forward URL for the specific Process API (PAPI) from the
Postgres database and routes the status update accordingly.

## ACL Data Model and Operations

The ACL database record acts as the source of truth connecting the following:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Element</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Identification</p></td><td><p><code>client_id</code>, <code>org_id</code>, <code>actionId</code>, and <code>actionVersion</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Routing</p></td><td><p>The callback PAPI URL specific to that document type</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Status</p></td><td><p>An indicator to enable or disable the record</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!IMPORTANT] Limit access to the ACL table to the operations team. Implement a database trigger to
> log every Insert, Update, and Delete operation in an ACL_Log table for auditing.

## Reviewer and Access Governance

Follow these practices to control who can access and review documents in your IDP implementation:

- Assign User Group permissions

  Use CloudHub User Groups rather than individual accounts when assigning reviewers during the
  design phase.

- Manage identity access

  Control these groups through an identity platform such as IIQ with the team manager as the
  approver.

- Restrict IP access

  Apply IP allow-listing policies to your downstream Process APIs (PAPIs) to ensure they accept
  requests only from the XAPI.

## See Also

- [Data Extraction Best Practices](data-extraction-best-practices)
- [IDP Permissions](permissions)
- [Analyzing Documents Using Pre-Built Schemas](creating-document-actions)
---

# Supported Models

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Einstein supports these predictive multimodal models:

- OpenAIâs `GPT-4o (gpt-4o)` LLM
- OpenAIâs `GPT-4o Mini (gpt-4o-mini)` LLM
- OpenAIâs `GPT-5.1` LLM
- OpenAIâs `GPT-5.2` LLM
- Googleâs `GEMINI-2.0 Flash 001` LLM
- Googleâs `GEMINI-2.5 Flash` LLM
- Anthropicâs `Claude Sonnet 4.6` LLM

> [!WARNING] **GPT-4o and GPT-4o Mini retire in September 21, 2026**
>
> To prevent service interruption, migrate actions to a supported model by September 21, 2026.

When creating a document action, you can select the model and extraction settings such as PII
masking or image recognition. Each model performs differently under various conditions, so select
the one that aligns with your specific requirements.

For additional details on GPT models' lifecycle and deprecation, see
[Microsoft Foundry Models lifecycle and support policy](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/foundry/openai/concepts/model-retirements?view=foundry-classic&tabs=text).

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Model Name</th><th>Description</th><th>Stability</th><th>Image Recognition</th><th>Location Callouts</th><th>Notes</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Einstein OpenAI GPT-4o</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Suitable for most tasks, performing well on documents in non-Latin languages. Can compare font sizes and identify certain font styles.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>High</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Standard performance</p></li><li><p>Low accuracy on reading checkboxes in forms</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Not supported</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Ask the model to not make up information to prevent hallucinations</p></li><li><p>List of <a href="https://help.openai.com/en/articles/8357869-how-to-change-your-language-setting-in-chatgpt#h_513834920e">languages supported</a> by OpenAI models</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Einstein OpenAI GPT-4o Mini</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Fast and useful for focused tasks. Tends to exhibit lazy reasoning.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>High</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Standard performance</p></li><li><p>Low accuracy on reading checkboxes in forms</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Not supported</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Ask the model to not make up information to prevent hallucinations</p></li><li><p>List of <a href="https://help.openai.com/en/articles/8357869-how-to-change-your-language-setting-in-chatgpt#h_513834920e">languages supported</a> by OpenAI models</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Einstein OpenAI GPT-5.1</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Suitable for most tasks. Improved instruction following and accuracy compared to GPT-4o.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>High</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Medium accuracy on reading checkboxes in forms</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Not supported</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Meets EU data residency requirements</p></li><li><p>Ask the model to not make up information to prevent hallucinations</p></li><li><p>List of <a href="https://help.openai.com/en/articles/8357869-how-to-change-your-language-setting-in-chatgpt#h_513834920e">languages supported</a> by OpenAI models</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Einstein OpenAI GPT-5.2</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Suitable for documents with charts, tables, and complex layouts due to improved visual understanding.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>High</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Medium accuracy on reading checkboxes in forms</p></li><li><p>Performs slightly better than earlier GPT models when reading checkboxes and tables</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Not supported</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Does not currently meet EU data residency requirements<br>For updates on data residency requirements, see <a href="https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=ai.generative_ai_large_language_model_support.htm&amp;type=5">Salesforceâs Large Language Models Support</a></p></li><li><p>Ask the model to not make up information to prevent hallucinations</p></li><li><p>List of <a href="https://help.openai.com/en/articles/8357869-how-to-change-your-language-setting-in-chatgpt#h_513834920e">languages supported</a> by OpenAI models</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Einstein GEMINI-2.0 Flash 001</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Good for analyzing images due to increased accuracy in this type of documents.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Standard</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Medium accuracy on reading checkboxes in forms when processing one page at a time</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Supported</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>List of <a href="https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/13575153?hl=en">languages supported</a> by Gemini models</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Einstein GEMINI-2.5 Flash</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Good for analyzing images due to increased accuracy in this type of document. Performs quicker and with higher accuracy than Einstein GEMINI-2.0 Flash 001.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Standard</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Medium accuracy on reading checkboxes in forms when processing one page at a time</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Supported</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>List of <a href="https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/13575153?hl=en">languages supported</a> by Gemini models</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Einstein Anthropic Claude Sonnet 4.6</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Good for general document extraction tasks requiring a balance of accuracy and speed. Performs well on structured and semi-structured documents with complex or varied layouts.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>High</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Performs better than Einstein OpenAI GPT-5.2 on handwritten documents</p></li><li><p>Medium accuracy on reading checkboxes in forms</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Not supported</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Supports up to 16 fields per document action</p></li><li><p>Ask the model to not make up information to prevent hallucinations</p></li><li><p>List of <a href="https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/multilingual-support">languages supported</a> by Anthropic models</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Model Errors

In some scenarios, the LLMs stop generating a response and provide an error code to understand the
reason that caused this behavior. If the error occurs during the execution of a document action, IDP
provides this error in the `statusMessage` attribute of the response object.

These are some of the most common error codes and their suggested fixes:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Error Code</th><th>Error Description</th><th>Suggested Fix</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><code>length</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The request exceeded the maximum input token limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>See <a href="https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/text">Using long context</a> under GPT-4.1 prompting best practices to understand how to refine your prompts.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>MAX_TOKENS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The response exceeded the maximum output token limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Split the document or select an OpenAI model for this extraction.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>SPII</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The document potentially contains Sensitive Personally Identifiable Information (SPII).</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Delete the conflicting field, or use GPT models to abstract the information.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

For a complete list of error codes, refer to each of the model providerâs documentation:

- OpenAI models:
  [The completion object](https://developers.openai.com/api/reference/resources/completions)

  Select **Show properties** under **choices** to see the details.

- Google models:
  [Finish Reason](https://docs.cloud.google.com/python/docs/reference/vertexai/latest?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

## See Also

- [OpenAIâs Prompt Engineering Guide](https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/text)
- [Analyzing Documents With Custom User-Defined Schemas](analyzing-documents-with-einstein)
